nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; steep outer reef slopes, deep lagoons, and channel walls in current-prone habitats, typically 12–45 m depth (range 0–60 m), often associated with gorgonians and sea whips

diet.notes: In the wild, feeds primarily on zooplankton (trophic level ~2.7). In captivity, generally accepts a varied diet of meaty foods (mysis, krill, brine shrimp), herbivore preparations, and quality flake or pellet foods. Feed multiple times daily.

summary: A bright lemon-yellow damselfish from the Western Pacific that grows larger than most damsels (~12 cm). Hardy and generally beginner-friendly, but becomes more territorial with age and is best kept singly.
careNotes: The Yellow Damselfish (Amblyglyphidodon aureus), also sold as Golden Damselfish or Giant Gold Damsel, is one of the larger members of its genus and a hardy aquarium choice. Juveniles display vivid lemon-yellow coloration sometimes accented by faint neon-blue racing stripes, which generally fade as the fish matures and grows more assertive. While it is commonly considered reef-safe and generally does not harass sessile invertebrates, its conspecific aggression and tendency toward territorial behavior become more pronounced with size. Sources disagree on adult size and tank requirements: LiveAquaria lists 3 in (7.6 cm) and a 30-gallon (~113 L) minimum tank, while FishBase reports up to 13 cm TL, ReefApp suggests 12 cm and a 300 L minimum, and Quality Marine recommends 70 gal (~265 L) and up to 5 in. The larger figures align with the species' adult biology, and a tank of about 300 L is advised to accommodate territorial behavior, swimming space, and rockwork. Temperature: retailers commonly cite 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C); FishBase reports a tropical wild range of about 26–29°C; an averaged target of roughly 24–27°C is reasonable. Provide ample live rock with caves for shelter alongside open swimming space. Generally docile when small but is best kept singly in most home aquariums; multiple conspecifics rarely coexist peacefully outside very large systems where they can be introduced simultaneously.
breedingNotes: Oviparous with distinct pair bonding during breeding. In the wild, males prepare and guard demersal egg patches laid on dead gorgonian branches and aerate them until hatching. Captive breeding is rarely reported.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Used for water parameters (temperature 72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, salinity SG 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12), care level (easy/beginner), temperament (semi-aggressive), diet, and reef compatibility. LiveAquaria lists adult size as 3 in (7.6 cm), which conflicts with multiple other sources reporting 12–13 cm; the larger figure was preferred. The collection landing page provided as the manifest primary URL (/collections/damselfish) did not link to a species page directly; this product page (/product/132) was located via search and used as primary.

additional.name: BlueZoo Aquatics
additional.notes: Cross-checked scientific name, native range (Coral Sea, Fiji), care level (beginner), temperament (semi-aggressive), diet (omnivore), reef safety (yes), and trade names (Golden Damselfish, Giant Gold Damsel). Lists 5 in adult size and 30 gal minimum tank.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (Pomacentridae), max size (13.0 cm TL), depth range (0–60 m, typical 12–45 m), wild distribution (Western Pacific from Andaman Sea/Christmas Island east to Fiji, north to Ryukyu Islands, south to Rowley Shoals/New Caledonia), wild temperature (26.1–29°C), trophic level (2.7, zooplanktivore), and reproductive behavior (oviparous, male egg-guarding on gorgonians).

additional.name: ReefApp
additional.notes: Cross-checked native range and provided a higher minimum tank recommendation (300 L / ~79 gal) and adult size (12 cm). Notes that the species can become territorial/aggressive, especially toward conspecifics; rates reef safety as 'with caution', diverging from LiveAquaria/BlueZoo which mark it reef-safe.

additional.name: Quality Marine
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (5 in / ~12.7 cm), water parameters (matching LiveAquaria), and recommends a larger minimum tank (70 gal / ~265 L). Confirms omnivorous diet and notes the species can become territorial with age.

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous pair-spawners; males guard and aerate demersal eggs on dead gorgonian branches in the wild. Captive breeding is rarely achieved.
